content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1106 S Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

December 24, 1975

Mr. Fred D. Corfee
Paine, Walker, Jackson & Curtis, Inc.
925 L Street, Suite 1400
Sacramento CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-215

Dear Mr. Corfee:

Thank you for your letter of December 8, 1975, requesting an opinion with regard to the Sacramento Republican Fund's (hereinafter referred to as the Fund) duties so file campaign statements. Because your letter raises no substantial litigation involving an interpretation of the Political Reform Act, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope that the following informal comments will be helpful.

Because the Fund contributed $1,500 to Friends of Mike Abernathy, the Fund was a "committee supporting ... a candidate" in the 4th Assembly District Special Election. Accordingly, the Fund must file campaign statements not later than thirty days prior to the primary, not later than seven days prior to the primary, not later than seven days prior to the runoff nomination and not later than twenty-five days after the runoff election. Gov. Code Section 84201. Thus, the Fund should have filed campaign statements not later than October 3, 1975, covering the period from July 1 (or January ?) through September ?, not later than October 28, 1975, covering the period for September 30 through October 2; not later than November 25, 1975, covering the period from October 26 through November 22, and not later than February 5, 1976, covering the period from November 23, 1975, through January 2, 1976.

Your letter indicates that on October 6, the Fund contributed $1,500 to Friends of Mike Abernathy and filed a campaign statement reporting that expenditure on November 20, 1975. Applying the filing requirements of Section 84202 to these facts, you must file two additional campaign statements for the 4th Assembly District races. One statement was due not later than November 25, and should have covered the period from the closing date of the statement filed on November 20 through November 22. In addition, you must file a postelection statement not later than February 5, 1976. You may file the postelection statement prior to that date if all liabilities to the Fund have been paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are anticipated with respect to the 4th Assembly District election. Gov. Code Section 84205.

Mr. Fred D. Corfoa
December 24, 1975
Page two


The next campaign statement filed by the Sacramento
Republican Fund, assuming the Fund does not make any expendi-
tures to support candidates or measures in intervening elec-
tions must be filed by July 31, 1976. The statement must cover
the period from the closing date of a posteelection statement
through June 30, 1976. Gov. Code Section 84206.

The Commission is considering possible amendments for
recommendation to the Legislature to simplify the filing require-
ments. Any suggestions you have will be welcome.

If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West,
a staff attorney.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DHL:pvp

However, I hope that the following informal comments will be helpful. Because the Fund contributed $1,500 to Friends of Mike Abernathy, the Fund was a "committee supporting ... a candidate" in the 4th Assembly District Special Election. Accordingly, the Fund must file campaign statements not later than thirty-three days prior to the primary, not later than seven days prior to the primary, not later than seven days prior to the runoff election and not later than sixty-five days after the runoff election. Government Code Section 84201. Thus, the Fund should have filed campaign statements not later than October 1, 1975, covering the period from July 1 (or January 7) through September 30; not later than October 28, 1975, covering the period from September 30 through October 25; not later than November 25, 1975, covering the period from October 26 through November 22, and not later than February 5, 1976, covering the period from November 23, 1975, through January 24, 1976. Your letter indicates that on October 6, the Fund contributed $1,500 to Friends of Mike Abernathy and filed a campaign statement reporting that expenditure on November 20, 1975. Applying the filing requirements of Section 84201 to these facts, you must file two additional campaign statements for the 4th Assembly District race. One statement was due not later than November 25, and should have covered the period from the closing date of the statement filed on November 20 through November 22. In addition, you must file a postelection statement not later than February 5, 1976. You may file the postelection statement prior to that date if all liabilities to the Fund have been paid and no additional contributions or expenditures are anticipated with respect to the 4th Assembly District election. Gov. Code Section 84205. Mr. Fred D. Corfee December 24, 1975 Page Two The next campaign statement filed by the Sacramento Republican Fund, assuming the Fund does not make any expenditures to support candidates or measures in intervening elections must be filed by July 31, 1976.
